chunks.text: Christopher Hunn is a Lecturer at UC Berkeley's EECS department. He earned a Bachelor's in Cognitive Science (Neuroscience Track) from UC Berkeley, a Biological Sciences Education credential from Cal State East Bay, and has done doctoral-level coursework in Policy Organization Measurement and Evaluation at Berkeley. He started work in the EECS department at UC Berkeley in 2012, and worked his way up from Graduate Student Advisor to CS Advisor and Undergraduate Affairs Supervisor, then to Director of Undergraduate Affairs in Computer Science, and finally to EECS Director of Undergraduate Instruction in 2017. Before coming to work at Berkeley, Hunn spent five years at the West Contra Costa Unified School District from 2005 to 2009, and was an instructor and curriculum developer at NASA Ames Research Center from 2000 to 2002.
chunks.question: What is Christopher Hunn's educational and professional background?
chunks.answer: He has a Bachelor's in Cognitive Science and worked at NASA and schools.

chunks.text: Christopher Hunn teaches CS 365: Introduction to Instructional Methods in Computer Science for Academic Interns on Fridays from 10:00-11:59 in Soda 438. He also teaches CS 370-2: Adaptive Instruction Methods in Computer Science on Thursdays from 19:00-20:59 in Dwinelle 182.
chunks.question: What courses does Christopher Hunn teach and when?
chunks.answer: CS 365 on Fridays and CS 370-2 on Thursdays.
